name: omd-orbit-admission
purpose: Own deterministic, deadline-bounded admission of overlapping path leases while preserving OMD fencing and disjoint-write safety.

fit_evidence: All consumers require one authoritative conflict decision over shared HELD and PENDING state.
Queue order, deadlines, cancellation, crash recovery, deadlock prevention, and fencing must survive adapter and process boundaries.
The current HELD-only admission rule differs from PENDING promotion and reproduced overtaking in every locked M0 trace.
alternatives_considered: Patch Coordinator.claim with one PENDING scan
Reuse the single-resource semaphore queue unchanged
Replace SQLite before separating admission mechanism from Git effects

boundary.owns: OrbitRequest identity, compatible-mode conflict decisions, reservation order, lease grant facts, wait deadlines, cancellation, and promotion authority
boundary.mechanism: Normalize a request, derive a transaction-local maximal-literal-prefix candidate superset from the complete HELD/PENDING snapshot, exact-check candidate HELD and predecessor PENDING blockers, reject reservation cycles or overload, and atomically grant or queue
non_goals: Running Git, checkers, notifications, or wall-clock reads inside the pure decision kernel
Treating all PENDING requests as one global FIFO head
Using a candidate index result as the final overlap oracle
boundary.state_authority: SQLite LeaseAuthority is the single mutable authority; one atomic authority transaction serializes decisions that may conflict within a repository

state_model: A pure admission decision table owns grant-versus-queue-versus-deny; spec/scheduler_admission_fsm.json owns each OrbitRequest lifecycle orthogonally to the existing Task FSM. The runtime_projection below is the only compatibility mapping to the legacy Orbit FSM.

decision_kernel.interface: decide(repository_snapshot, normalized_request, recorded_policy, observed_at) -> Grant | Queue | Deny | Reject
decision_kernel.rank: For the active v2 policy, age=max(0, observed_at-enqueued_at), boost=min(max_age_boost, floor(age/aging_quantum)), and effective_priority=base_priority+boost; new v2 base priority must leave signed-64 headroom for the configured ceiling. Rank is effective_priority descending then durable queue_seq ascending. Legacy v1 rows receive boost zero, and rank is compared only among exact conflicting PENDING requests.
decision_kernel.grant: No incompatible exact HELD blocker and no higher-ranked exact conflicting PENDING predecessor exist.
decision_kernel.queue: At least one blocker exists, capacity is available, the wait deadline is valid, and inserting reservation edges does not close a cycle.
decision_kernel.deny: The request is valid but policy or reservation-cycle safety forbids admission.
decision_kernel.reject: The request identity, mode, pathspec, TTL, deadline, or capacity envelope is invalid before a live wait is exposed.
decision_kernel.output_identity: Every decision binds the complete request identity, decision_id, authority_snapshot_hash, policy_version, base_priority, effective_priority, observed_at, and the queue or grant context committed by that decision.
decision_kernel.candidate_filter: The derived literal-prefix trie may only reduce exact glob comparisons. It is rebuilt from the complete active snapshot, places first-segment wildcard and character-class rows in the global candidate set, falls back to all active rows for an unknown request prefix or any build/query error, and never supplies rank, cycle, state, or snapshot authority.
decision_kernel.decision_id: SHA-256 of the canonical admission_decision/v2 envelope: event type, the complete nine-field request identity, authority_snapshot_hash, base_priority, effective_priority, observed_at, and the decision-specific grant, queue, denial, rejection, or blocker fields. Exact replay is idempotent; same id with a different envelope is a rejected security conflict; uniqueness scope is repository_id/request_id/request_generation.

protocol.versioning: Request, policy, event, snapshot, queue rank, and path-normalization versions are recorded. Breaking compatibility or ordering changes require a new major version and replay fixtures.

invariants: Two live HELD requests whose modes are incompatible never have overlapping pathspecs.
A new request is granted only when it has no incompatible HELD blocker and no higher-ranked conflicting PENDING predecessor.
Queue precedence is defined only between conflicting requests; unrelated PENDING work never blocks a disjoint request.

The candidate index returns a superset of every exact overlap. Global-pattern authority rows are candidates for every query; request uncertainty or index construction/query failure falls back to the full active set.
A queue insertion that would close the combined HELD-plus-reservation wait-for cycle is denied before exposure.

Because aging can change reservation precedence after insertion, every reconciliation recomputes the combined graph at one observed time and deterministically denies the newest participating queue ticket until it is acyclic.
Every live lease has a unique monotonic fence and only its current owner, fence, and bail epoch may renew or release it.

Every admission and promotion decision binds the canonical nine-field request identity (repository, request, orbit, generation, owner, bail epoch, mode, pathspec digest, policy version), decision-specific output fields, decision_id, and a fresh authority snapshot; lifecycle maintenance facts bind the applicable owner/fence/deadline tuple. An event for another identity or generation cannot advance this lifecycle.

Owner reclaim of a HELD request requires the current owner, fence, bail epoch, request generation, and authority snapshot; reclaim of a PENDING request requires the current owner, bail epoch, request generation, and authority snapshot.
Every wait has a durable sequence, deadline, cancellation path, and bounded capacity outcome.
Promotion and initial admission use the same blocker, compatibility, rank, and cycle rules.

runtime.ordering: Authority mutations are serialized per repository. Queue rank is effective_priority descending then queue_seq ascending, evaluated under one recorded observed time. The active content-addressed v2 envelope (default 60-second quantum and +10 ceiling, operational rather than proof-derived) is pinned in repository DB metadata; drift or corruption fails before recovery.
runtime.concurrency: Disjoint or mode-compatible requests may become HELD in the same promotion pass and execute concurrently outside the authority transaction.
runtime.backpressure: The current one-repository-per-DB authority pins one durable non-negative queue limit (default 1024; zero is no-wait) and returns a typed QUEUE_FULL receipt with retry_after_at metadata. Cross-repository global and conflict-domain limits remain deferred until their no-bypass accounting is proved.
runtime.cancellation: A PENDING owner may cancel with current identity and bail epoch. Cancellation is idempotent and immediately triggers eligible promotion.
runtime.timeouts: Wait and lease deadlines are persisted timestamps. Direct embedded Coordinator construction starts a 1-second authority tick when sweep_interval is omitted; explicit None or zero is the deterministic opt-out. Under singleton enforcement a separate lifecycle heartbeat worker refreshes the leader lease at leader_ttl/3 independently of slow sweep execution. MCP defers background sweep and outbox work until lifespan entry and CLI remains one-shot/inline. Every deadline event carries observed time; no lazy status read is the sole liveness mechanism. Finite aging boost does not prove starvation freedom; wait_deadline is the bounded-resolution authority.
runtime.notification_delivery: State-changing admission edges enqueue admission_notification/v1 in the same authority transaction. A post-commit hook only arms an asynchronous dispatcher; notifier I/O holds neither authority nor repository-effect locks. Delivery is leased, claim-token fenced, bounded by a finite attempt timeout and a maximum live-plus-retained-outcome registry, per request-generation FIFO, and deduplicatable at-least-once by stable event_id. Bounded late-outcome eviction may replay the same identity. available_at and claim_deadline autonomously re-arm delivery even when deadline sweep is disabled; replacement timer start is retried three times without discarding an existing later timer. A poisoned stream does not block another request stream while notifier capacity remains. Manual flush and timer delivery share a lifecycle gate, and close rejects new drains then joins accepted drains and strict effects before handoff. Legacy fail-soft telemetry is deferred until commit and authority-lock release, preventing sink reentrancy from inverting dispatcher and authority locks. A coordination_notification/v1 agent-reclaim summary is causally blocked on every released-orbit predecessor, or uses a synthetic stream when no orbit exists. PROMOTION_BLOCKED and RENEW remain non-durable high-rate observations in this bounded slice.

durability.persistence: Persist normalized request identity, queue sequence, requested TTL, deadlines, content-addressed policy version, rank inputs, decision schema/observed time/effective priority, blockers, state, and fence before reporting a durable result.
durability.atomic_commit: Commit dedup claim, request state/version, wait-for cycle decision, fence allocation or queue insertion, notification outbox, and any explicit cross-stream causal predecessors in one authority transaction.
durability.recovery: On restart, first reconcile authority state by expiring due waits and leases and reevaluating PENDING requests with their original queue sequence and policy envelope; then arm the asynchronous dispatcher, which reclaims expired notification delivery leases and replays due stream heads by stable event_id. The candidate index has no persisted authority or stale recovery state: every decision rebuilds it from that decision's complete active snapshot.
durability.effect_idempotency: Use repository_id, request_id, orbit_id, transition kind, and request generation as the unique semantic effect key; the canonical payload has a separate digest, and the same effect key with a different event or payload is a fail-closed conflict.
durability.migration: The schema version is the final completion marker for additive migration. The m1-aging predecessor had no notification facts, so migration creates an empty outbox without fabricating historical effects while preserving all authority rows. A crash after outbox DDL or row insertion but before the version marker resumes idempotently and preserves the exact existing payload, digest, and dependency rows. Migration tests retain legacy PENDING, HELD, RELEASED, EXPIRED, and DENIED authority states.

runtime_projection.legacy_target: omd_server/fsm.py Orbit FSM plus the current orbits table

state_map.REQUESTED: transient; no legacy orbit row is externally visible
state_map.REJECTED: no live legacy row; return a typed rejection receipt
state_map.CANCELLED: DENIED with typed reason=cancelled during compatibility mode
state_map.TIMED_OUT: DENIED with typed reason=wait_timeout during compatibility mode

field_map.request_id: dedup request identity and admission receipt
field_map.queue_seq: new durable queue_seq; legacy created_at ordering is read-only migration input, never the M1 authority
field_map.wait_deadline: new durable wait_deadline; legacy PENDING rows receive the explicit deterministic migration policy (created/enqueued time plus the legacy bounded wait window) before M1 authority
field_map.request_generation: new monotonic request generation used with request_id and orbit_id
field_map.authority_snapshot_hash: new digest of the authority rows and coordinator epoch used by the decision
field_map.bail_epoch: orbits.bail_epoch and the canonical request identity
field_map.pathspec_digest: canonical normalized pathspec digest persisted with the request
field_map.policy_version: content-addressed admission policy version; the active canonical envelope is pinned per repository DB
field_map.base_priority: orbits.priority in the signed-64-bit priority domain
field_map.effective_priority: orbits.decision_effective_priority for the last admission/promotion observation
field_map.observed_at: orbits.decision_observed_at, shared by rank, snapshot and decision within one authority transaction
field_map.decision_id: canonical admission_decision/v2 digest resolving to the atomically persisted decision envelope

runtime_projection.rule: Projection is one-way compatibility output. Production conformance compares the semantic lifecycle first and then the legacy projection; legacy state names must not redefine the semantic machine. task_id is non-identity scheduling metadata and is deliberately excluded from the canonical request identity.

failure_model: Invalid pathspec, mode, TTL, priority, or identity is rejected without state mutation.
Capacity exhaustion returns explicit overload rather than creating an unbounded PENDING row.
Reservation-cycle detection denies the candidate request and preserves prior grants and queue order.
Unknown pending policy authority blocks fail closed; malformed or drifted active policy metadata prevents startup before recovery.
Candidate-index disable, an unknown request prefix, malformed active pathspec, coverage failure, or build/query error uses the complete active set and the unchanged exact oracle.
Stale owner, fence, or bail epoch cannot renew, release, or cancel a current request.
Notification failure, child-thread BaseException, or notifier-start failure leaves an observable retryable outbox entry and does not roll back an accepted lease fact.
A closed coordinator rejects new manual drains and cannot ACK or retry durable notification state after handoff.
A transient dispatcher timer-start failure is retried without losing an already armed later wake; exhausting the finite bound is explicitly observable.

Default embedded authority and separate heartbeat workers hold only weak Coordinator references, wake immediately on owner collection, and stop on lost leadership. The heartbeat remains live while close joins accepted sweep/notifier effects, then close stops it behind a terminal lifecycle gate before leadership handoff. Worker-start failure closes and resigns. Server construction and one-shot CLI commands explicitly suppress pre-lifecycle background writers and notifier effects; synchronous schema initialization and recovery may still occur.
Legacy sink reentrancy cannot run while the authority transaction or RLock is held.
An agent-reclaim summary cannot be delivered before any released-orbit event it summarizes.

traces: request_id, orbit_id, task_id, repository, agent, mode, pathspec digest, queue_seq, base and effective priority, observed_at, content-addressed policy version, blocker ids, decision, fence, deadlines, transition, correlation id
candidate-index version, complete HELD/PENDING counts, selected HELD/PENDING counts, full-scan fallback and bounded fallback reason
metrics: notification backlog, attempts, delivery lease age, and retry lag
observability.redaction: Path content and unbounded identities remain trace fields or hashes; metrics use bounded repository and mode labels.

security: Lease owner, cancellation, and fence capabilities are explicit and never inferred from caller-supplied task text.
A caller cannot supply its own candidate set, effective rank, cycle result, or fence.
Repository-domain traffic cannot alter another repository's queue rank, capacity, or candidate-index residency.
verification: Validate the engine, lifecycle FSM, and abstract traces.
Run a decision-table truth suite for every mode pair, HELD blocker, PENDING predecessor, priority, FIFO, aging, capacity, and cycle combination.
Property-test indexed candidates against a full exact scan with the assertion full_conflicts is a subset of indexed_candidates.
Run real Coordinator positive readback and injected-negative OOPTDD receipts for no-overtaking.

Crash-test queue insertion, grant, release-plus-promotion, cancellation, deadline, and notification cut points; verify omitted embedded default, explicit off, timeout-before-promotion, heartbeat independence from a blocked sweep, heartbeat retention through accepted-effect joins, worker-start rollback, terminal close/start linearization, context-manager resign, immediate weak-reference worker wakeup, actual server outbox deferral, CLI opt-out, and authority/outbox timer separation.
Model-check bounded safety and liveness with conflicting partial orders and restart.
implementation_slices: M1a: add metrics and a pure compatibility/rank/blocker decision table without changing production outcomes.

M1b (implemented): persist queue_seq, requested TTL, wait deadline, policy version, cancellation, and repository capacity; enforce no-overtaking with the locked gate and return typed overload without a live row.

M1c (implemented): add PENDING reservation edges plus deterministic dynamic-cycle resolution, a content-addressed saturating-aging v2 policy, one-clock decision evidence, schema migration, and restart/property tests.

M1d (implemented): persist state-changing semantic admission notifications and coordination dependencies transactionally; deliver them post-commit through lifecycle-gated, timer-start-fenced, claim-token-fenced, per-stream FIFO at-least-once replay without changing accepted lease facts.

M2a-index (implemented): derive a conservative maximal-literal-prefix candidate trie from every complete authority snapshot, retain exact glob verification and full-scan fallback, and property/differential-test it against the full oracle.

M2a-deadline (implemented): make embedded-runtime authority ticks lifecycle-safe by default with singleton heartbeat, retain explicit deterministic opt-out, keep server construction background-worker-free until its lifespan, keep CLI one-shot, and prove that notification timers do not create deadline transitions.
M2b: move all Git effects out of the authority transaction before reconsidering RLock or SQLite replacement.
falsifiers: A newer lower-ranked conflicting request becomes HELD while its predecessor remains PENDING.
A global queue head blocks a request that is disjoint and mode-compatible with all live blockers.
The indexed path misses any conflict found by the full exact scan.
A bounded workload can leave a PENDING request without grant, timeout, cancellation, denial, or explicit overload.
Adding reservation precedence introduces an undetected wait-for cycle.
The abstraction should collapse back into a module if only one caller remains and durable queue, recovery, and replaceable policies disappear.
